help_text:
The uncompromising code formatter.
Usage: black [OPTIONS] SRC ...

Reformat the given SRC files or all files under SRC in place.

Options:
  --check               Don't write the files back, just return the status.
  --diff                Don't write the files back, just output a diff.
  --line-length INTEGER How many characters per line to allow.  [default: 88]
  --target-version [py33|py34|py35|py36|py37|py38|py39|py310]
                        Python versions that should be supported by Black's output.
  --help                Show this message and exit.

isort - The Python code import sorter.
Usage: isort [OPTIONS] [FILES]...

Sort Python import declarations alphabetically and automatically separate them into sections.
Isort will overwrite contents of files in place.

Options:
  --check-only, -c      Don't actually write to the file(s).
  --diff                Show diff output.
  --line-length INTEGER, -l INTEGER
                        The max length of an import line.  [default: 79]
  --help                Show this message and exit.

usage: autopep8 [-h] [--version] [-v] [-d] [-i] [--global-config filename]
                [--ignore-local-config] [-r] [-j n] [-p n] [-a] [--experimental]
                [--exclude globs] [--list-fixes] [--ignore errors]
                [--select errors] [--max-line-length n] [--line-range line line]
                [--indent-size n]
                [files [files ...]]

Automatically formats Python code to conform to the PEP 8 style guide.

positional arguments:
  files                 files to format or '-' for standard in

optional arguments:
  -i, --in-place        make changes in-place
  -d, --diff            print the diff for the fixed source code
  --help                show this help message and exit

Usage: flake8 [options] file file ...

A tool that glues together pycodestyle, pyflakes, mccabe, and third-party plugins
to check the style and quality of Python code.

Options:
  --version             show program's version number and exit
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --filename=FILENAME_PATTERNS
                        Only check for filenames matching the patterns in this
                        comma-separated list
  --max-line-length=n   Maximum allowed line length for the entirety of this run
  --select=ERRORS       Comma-separated list of errors/warnings to enable
  --ignore=ERRORS       Comma-separated list of errors and warnings to ignore
  --count                Print total number of errors and warnings to standard
                        output and set the exit code to 1 if total is not null

usage: pylint [options]

Pylint is a static code analysis tool which looks for programming errors,
helps enforcing a coding standard, sniffs for code smells and offers simple
refactoring suggestions.

Options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --version             show program's version number and exit
  --disable=<msg ids>   Disable the message, report, category or checker
                        with the given id(s).
  --enable=<msg ids>    Enable the message, report, category or checker
                        with the given id(s).
  --output-format=<format>
                        Set the output format. Available formats are: text,
                        parseable, colorized, json, msvs.
  --errors-only, -E     In error mode, messages with a category besides ERROR
                        or FATAL are suppressed.

usage: mypy [-h] [-v] [-V] [more options; see below]
            [-m MODULE] [-p PACKAGE] [-c PROGRAM_TEXT] [files ...]

Mypy is a static type checker for Python.

optional arguments:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  -V, --version         Show program's version number and exit
  --ignore-missing-imports
                        Silently ignore imports of missing modules
  --strict              Strict mode; enables the following flags
  --disallow-untyped-defs
                        Disallow defining functions without type annotations
  --check-untyped-defs  Check the bodies of functions without type annotations
  --no-error-summary    Do not show error summary
  --show-error-codes    Show error codes in error messages

usage: bandit [-h] [-r] [-a {file,vuln}] [-n CONTEXT_LINES] [-c CONFIG_FILE]
              [-p PROFILE] [-t TESTS] [-s SKIPS] [-l] [-i]
              [-f {csv,custom,html,json,screen,txt,xml,yaml}]
              [--msg-template MSG_TEMPLATE] [-o OUTPUT_FILE] [-v] [-d]
              [-q] [--ignore-nosec] [-x SKIPS] [-b BASELINE]
              [--ini INI_PATH] [--version]
              [targets [targets ...]]

Bandit - a Python source code security linter

positional arguments:
  targets               source file(s) or directory(s) to be tested

optional arguments:
  -r, --recursive       find and process files in subdirectories
  --severity-level {all,low,medium,high}
                        report only issues of a given severity level or higher

usage: pytest [options] [file_or_dir] [file_or_dir] [...]

pytest is a testing framework that makes it easy to write small, readable tests.

general:
  -v, --verbose         increase verbosity
  -q, --quiet           decrease verbosity
  --tb=style            traceback print mode (auto/long/short/line/native/no)
  -x, --exitfirst       exit instantly on first error or failed test
  -s                    shortcut for --capture=no
  --collect-only, --co  only collect tests, do not execute them
  --junitxml=path       create junit-xml style report file at path
  --html=path           create html report at path
  -k EXPRESSION         only run tests which match the given substring expression

Coverage.py, version 7.3.2
Measure, collect, and report on code coverage in Python programs.

usage: coverage <command> [options] [args]

Commands:
    annotate    Annotate source files with execution information.
    combine     Combine a number of data files.
    debug       Display information about the internals of coverage.py
    erase       Erase previously collected coverage data.
    html        Create an HTML report.
    json        Create a JSON report of coverage results.
    lcov        Create an LCOV report of coverage results.
    report      Report coverage stats on modules.
    run         Run a Python program and measure code coverage.
    xml         Create an XML report of coverage results.

Use "coverage help <command>" for help on any command.

Usage: yt-dlp [OPTIONS] URL [URL...]

A feature-rich command-line audio/video downloader.

General Options:
  -h, --help                      Print this help text and exit
  --version                       Print program version and exit
  -U, --update                    Update this program to the latest version
  -i, --ignore-errors             Continue on download errors
  -o, --output TEMPLATE           Output filename template
  --paths [TYPES:]PATH            The paths where the files should be downloaded to
  -f, --format FORMAT             Video format code
  --audio-format FORMAT           Format to convert the audio to
  --download-archive FILE         Download only videos not listed in the archive file

usage: gallery-dl [OPTION]... URL...

Download image-galleries and -collections from several image hosting sites.

General Options:
  -h, --help            Print this help message and exit
  -v, --verbose         Print various debugging information
  -q, --quiet           Activate quiet mode
  -d, --dest DEST       Destination directory
  -f, --filename FILENAME
                        Filename format string for downloaded files
  --cookies FILE        File to load/store cookies from/to
  --sleep N             Number of seconds to sleep between requests
  --rate RATE           Maximum download rate (e.g. 500k or 2.5M)

GNU Wget 1.21.4, a non-interactive network retriever.
Usage: wget [OPTION]... [URL]...

Mandatory arguments to long options are mandatory for short options too.

Startup:
  -V,  --version           display the version of Wget and exit.
  -h,  --help              print this help.

Logging and input file:
  -o,  --output-file=FILE    log messages to FILE.
  -q,  --quiet               quiet (no output).
  -nv, --no-verbose          turn off verbose without being quiet.
  -i,  --input-file=FILE     download URLs found in local or external FILE.

Download:
  -t,  --tries=NUMBER        set number of retries to NUMBER (0 unlimits).
  -O,  --output-document=FILE write documents to FILE.
  -nc, --no-clobber          skip downloads that would download to existing files.
  -c,  --continue            resume getting a partially-downloaded file.

usage: twine upload [-h] [-r REPOSITORY] [--repository-url REPOSITORY_URL]
                    [-s] [--sign-with SIGN_WITH] [-i IDENTITY] [-u USERNAME]
                    [-p PASSWORD] [--non-interactive] [-c COMMENT]
                    [--config-file CONFIG_FILE] [--skip-existing]
                    [--cert path] [--client-cert path] [--verbose] [--disable-progress-bar]
                    dist [dist ...]

Upload one or more distributions to a repository.

positional arguments:
  dist                  The distribution files to upload to the repository,
                        may additionally contain a .asc file to include an
                        existing signature.

optional arguments:
  -r REPOSITORY, --repository REPOSITORY
                        The repository (package index) to upload the package to.

usage: pip install [-h] [-r requirements] [-c constraints] [--no-deps]
                   [--pre] [-e] [-t TARGET] [--platform PLATFORM]
                   [--python-version PYTHON_VERSION]
                   [--implementation IMPLEMENTATION] [--abi ABI]
                   [--user] [--root ROOT] [--prefix PREFIX] [-b BUILD]
                   [--src SRC] [-U, --upgrade]
                   [--upgrade-strategy UPGRADE_STRATEGY] [--force-reinstall]
                   [--ignore-installed] [--ignore-requires-python]
                   [--no-build-isolation] [--use-pep517 | --no-use-pep517]
                   [--check-build-dependencies] [--break-system-packages]
                   package [package ...]

Install packages from PyPI, version control, local projects, and/or distribution files.

PIP_INDEX_URL and --index-url control which package index is used.

usage: img2pdf [-h] [-v] [-V] [-o out] [--version]
               [--without-pdfrw] [--first-frame-only]
               [-C colorspace] [-D]
               [-S LxW] [-s LxW] [-b L] [-m L]
               [-d IGNORE_OWN_METADATA] [-a]
               [images ...]

Losslessly convert raster images to PDF.

positional arguments:
  images                images to convert

optional arguments:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  -o out, --output out  output file (default: stdout)
  -V, --verbose         verbose output
  --first-frame-only    Only convert the first frame of multi-frame images

pandoc [OPTIONS] [FILES]
  -f FORMAT, -r FORMAT  --from=FORMAT, --read=FORMAT
  -t FORMAT, -w FORMAT  --to=FORMAT, --write=FORMAT
  -o FILENAME           --output=FILENAME
                        --data-dir=DIRECTORY
  -M KEY[:VALUE]        --metadata=KEY[:VALUE]
  -d FILE               --defaults=FILE
  --verbose             give verbose debugging output
  --quiet               suppress warnings
  --fail-if-warnings    exit with error status if there are any warnings
  --sandbox             run in sandbox, prohibiting access to files and the network

Input formats:  commonmark, creole, csv, docbook, docx, dokuwiki, epub, fb2,
                gfm, haddock, html, ipynb, jats, jira, json, latex, man,
Output formats: asciidoc, beamer, commonmark, context, csljson, docbook,
                docx, dokuwiki, dzslides, epub, fb2, gfm, haddock, html, ipynb

ffmpeg version 6.0 Copyright (c) 2000-2023 the FFmpeg developers
Usage: ffmpeg [options] [[infile options] -i infile]... {[outfile options] outfile}...

Print help / information / capabilities:
-L                  show license
-h                  show help
-? help             show help (alias for -h)
-formats            show available formats
-codecs             show available codecs

Audio options:
-aframes number     set the number of audio frames to output
-aq quality         set audio quality (codec-specific)
-ar rate            set audio sampling rate (in Hz)
-ac channels        set number of audio channels

Video options:
-vframes number     set the number of video frames to output
-vcodec codec       force video codec ('copy' to copy stream)
-r rate             set frame rate (Hz value, fraction or abbreviation)

pdftotext version 22.12.0
Copyright 2005-2022 The Poppler Developers - http://poppler.freedesktop.org
Copyright 1996-2011 Glyph & Cog, LLC
Usage: pdftotext [options] <PDF-file> [<text-file>]
  -f <int>          : first page to convert
  -l <int>          : last page to convert
  -r <fp>           : resolution, in DPI (default is 72)
  -x <int>          : x-coordinate of the crop area top left corner
  -y <int>          : y-coordinate of the crop area top left corner
  -W <int>          : width of crop area in pixels (default is 0)
  -H <int>          : height of crop area in pixels (default is 0)
  -layout           : maintain original physical layout
  -raw              : keep strings in content stream order
  -nodiag           : discard diagonal text
  -enc <string>     : output text encoding name (default is UTF-8)
  -eol <string>     : output end-of-line convention (unix, dos, or mac)
  -nopgbrk          : don't insert page breaks between pages

usage: pdf2txt.py [-h] [-d] [-p PAGENOS] [-m MAXPAGES] [-P PASSWORD] [-o OUTPUT]
                  [-C] [-n] [-A] [-V] [-M CHAR_MARGIN] [-L LINE_MARGIN]
                  [-W WORD_MARGIN] [-F BOXES_FLOW] [-Y LAYOUT_MODE]
                  [-O OUTPUT_DIR] [-R ROTATION] [-t OUTPUT_TYPE]
                  [-c INPUT_CODEC] [-s STRIP_CONTROL]
                  pdf_file [pdf_file ...]

PDF Miner text extractor. Extracts text and other content from PDF files.

positional arguments:
  pdf_file    PDF files to extract text from

optional arguments:
  -o OUTPUT, --output OUTPUT
                        Output file (default: stdout)
  -t OUTPUT_TYPE, --type OUTPUT_TYPE
                        Output type: text/html/xml/tag (default: text)

Usage: exiftool [OPTIONS] FILE
       exiftool [OPTIONS] -common_args [OPTIONS] FILE ...

ExifTool version 12.65

Reading options:
  --printConv (-p) Use print conversion (-p2 for special)
  --csv           Format tag output as CSV
  --json          Use JSON formatting for console output
  --xml           Use XML output format
  --list          List available tag names (-listf for file types)

Writing options:
  -TAG=VALUE      Write new VALUE for specified TAG
  -overwrite_original
                  Overwrite original (instead of preserving it)

ExifTool is a platform-independent Perl library plus a command-line application
for reading, writing and editing meta information in a wide variety of files.

usage: sphinx-build [OPTIONS] SOURCEDIR OUTPUTDIR [FILENAMES...]

Generate documentation from source files.

sphinx-build generates a Sphinx documentation set.
It is called like this:
  $ sphinx-build [options] sourcedir outdir [filenames]

where the 'sourcedir' is the 'source directory' from the documentation
configuration file, and 'outdir' is the directory in which you want to place
the built documentation.

Options:
  --version             show program's version number and exit
  -b BUILDER            builder to use (default: html)
  -a                    write all files (default: only write new and changed files)
  -E                    don't use a saved environment, always read all files
  -d PATH               path for the cached environment and doctree files
  -q                    no output on stdout, just warnings on stderr
  -W                    turn warnings into errors

Usage: mkdocs build [OPTIONS]

  Build the MkDocs documentation

Options:
  -c, --clean        Remove old files from the site directory before building.
  -f, --config-file FILENAME
                     Provide a specific MkDocs config
  -d, --site-dir PATH
                     The directory to output the result of the documentation build.
  --no-directory-urls
                     Disable the use of "pretty" URLs.
  -q, --quiet        Silence warnings
  -v, --verbose      Enable verbose output
  --help             Show this message and exit.

usage: summarize [OPTIONS] INPUT_FILE

Summarize text documents using extractive summarization.

Options:
  -n, --num-sentences INTEGER  Number of sentences in the summary  [default: 5]
  -o, --output PATH            Write summary to this file (default: stdout)
  --ratio FLOAT                Ratio of sentences to keep from original
  --language TEXT              Language of the input document  [default: english]
  --algorithm [luhn|lsa|lex_rank|edmundson|kl-sum|random|reduction]
                               Summarization algorithm to use  [default: luhn]
  --help                       Show this message and exit.

Usage: sumy [lex-rank|luhn|lsa|kl|random|edmundson|reduction] [--length=<n>] [--url=<url> | --file=<file>] [--format=<format>]

Automatic text summarizer.

Arguments:
  (lex-rank|luhn|lsa|kl|random|edmundson|reduction)
                              Summarization method

Options:
  --length=<n>                Length of summarization (sentences count) [default: 20%]
  --url=<url>                 URL address of summarized document, for example: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Automatic_summarization
  --file=<file>               Path to file with document to summarize, for example: CHANGES.rst
  --format=<format>           Format of input document, for example: html, plaintext, rst
  --language=<lang>           Natural language of summarized document, for example: english, chinese
  --stop-words=<file>         Path to a file with stop words

Ruff: An extremely fast Python linter and code formatter, written in Rust.

Usage: ruff [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Commands:
  check    Run Ruff on the given files or directories (default)
  rule     Explain a rule (or all rules)
  config   List or describe the available configuration options
  linter   List all supported upstream linters
  clean    Clear any caches in the current directory and any subdirectories
  format   Format Python files
  server   Run the language server
  version  Display Ruff's version
  help     Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
  -h, --help     Print help
  -V, --version  Print version

Usage: ruff format [OPTIONS] [FILES]...

Format Python files.

Formats Python files by rewriting them in place to conform to Ruff's code style.
Ruff's formatter is designed as a drop-in replacement for Black.

Options:
      --check       Avoid writing any formatted files back; instead, exit with a non-zero status code if any files would have been reformatted.
      --diff        Avoid writing any formatted files back; instead, exit with a non-zero status code and print a diff to stdout if any files would have been reformatted.
      --line-length <LINE_LENGTH>  Set the line length [default: 88]
  -h, --help        Print help

usage: pyinstaller [-h] [-v] [-D] [-F] [--specpath DIR] [-n NAME]
                   [--contents-directory CONTENTS_DIRECTORY]
                   [--add-data SOURCE:DEST] [--add-binary SOURCE:DEST]
                   [--hidden-import MODULENAME]
                   [--collect-submodules MODULENAME]
                   [--collect-data MODULENAME]
                   [--collect-binaries MODULENAME] [--collect-all MODULENAME]
                   [--copy-metadata PACKAGENAME]
                   [-p DIR] [-d {all,imports,bootloader,noarchive}] [--clean]
                   [--log-level LEVEL] [-y] [-c] [-w] [-i ICON] [--disable-windowed-traceback]
                   [--version-file FILE] [--manifest FILE]
                   [--no-embed-manifest] [--uac-admin] [--uac-uiaccess]
                   [scriptname [scriptname ...]]

Bundle a Python application and all its dependencies into a single package.

Package everything needed to run a Python app into a distributable bundle.

usage: nox [-h] [--version] [-l] [-s [SESSIONS]] [-k KEYWORDS] [-t TAGS]
           [-e PYTHONS] [--no-venv] [-r] [-R] [--no-reuse-existing-virtualenvs]
           [--nocolor] [--forcecolor] [-p] [--error-on-missing-interpreters]
           [--error-on-external-run] [--install-only] [--report OUTPUT]
           [--non-interactive] [--verbose] [-x] [--stop-on-first-error]
           [-P POSARGS] [--noisy-on-dry-run] [-f NOXFILE]
           [session [session ...]]

Flexible test automation for Python.

optional arguments:
  -l, --list-sessions   List available sessions and exit
  -s SESSIONS, --sessions SESSIONS
                        Run a set of sessions. Uses glob patterns.
  -k KEYWORDS           Only run sessions matching the given keywords
  -x, --stop-on-first-error
                        Stop after the first error

usage: tox [-h] [--version] [-v] [-q] [--listenvs] [--listenvs-all]
           [-e ENVLIST] [--skip-env ENVLIST_FILTER] [-c CONFIGFILE]
           [-i INDEXURL] [--pre] [--force-dep ARGS] [--skip-pkg-install]
           [--no-provision [REQUIRES_JSON]] [--devenv DEVENVDIR]
           [--runner {virtualenv,uv}]
           [args ...]

Run test suites in multiple Python environments.

optional arguments:
  -e ENVLIST, --env ENVLIST
                        comma-separated list of environments to run tests in
  -v, --verbose         increase verbosity
  -r, --recreate        recreate virtual environments if they exist
  --listenvs            show list of test environments (with description if verbose)
  --notest              Do not run the test commands in each env
  -c CONFIGFILE         config file name or directory with 'tox.ini',
                        'setup.cfg', or 'pyproject.toml'

usage: http [--json] [--form] [--multipart] [--boundary BOUNDARY]
            [--raw RAW] [--compress] [--pretty {all,colors,format,none}]
            [--style STYLE] [--unsorted-json] [--response-charset ENCODING]
            [--print WHAT] [--headers] [--meta] [--body] [--all]
            [--history-print WHAT] [--stream] [--output FILE]
            [--download] [--continue] [--quiet]
            [--session SESSION_NAME_OR_PATH] [--session-read-only ...]
            [--auth AUTH] [--auth-type {basic,bearer,digest,...}]
            [--ignore-stdin] [--offline] [--check-status] [--follow] [--max-redirects N]
            [METHOD] URL [REQUEST_ITEM ...]

HTTPie: modern, user-friendly command-line HTTP client for the API era.
Make HTTP requests and inspect/debug API responses.

HTTP method defaults to GET when no body is provided, POST otherwise.

Usage: check-version [OPTIONS]

Options:
  --help  Show this message and exit.

usage: mytool [-h] [-v]

optional arguments:
  -h, --help     show this help message and exit
  -v, --verbose  increase output verbosity

usage: framework [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...

  A general purpose orchestration framework.

Options:
  --config TEXT  Path to config file
  --env TEXT     Environment name
  --verbose      Enable verbose logging
  --dry-run      Show what would be done without doing it
  --help         Show this message and exit.

Commands:
  init      Initialize a new project
  status    Show current status
  validate  Validate configuration

usage: debugtool [-h] [--attach PID] [--port PORT] [--timeout TIMEOUT]
                  [--log-level {debug,info,warn,error}]
                  [--output-format {json,text,csv}]

Process diagnostic and introspection utility.

optional arguments:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --attach PID          Attach to running process by PID
  --port PORT           Port for debug server  [default: 5678]
  --timeout TIMEOUT     Connection timeout in seconds  [default: 30]
  --log-level           Set logging level
  --output-format       Output format for diagnostics

usage: pycodestyle [--version] [-v] [-q] [--first] [--show-source]
                   [--show-pep8] [--format=FORMAT] [--select=ERRORS]
                   [--ignore=ERRORS] [--max-line-length=n] [--max-doc-length=n]
                   [--indent-char=CHAR] [--exclude=PATTERNS] [--filename=PATTERNS]
                   [--statistics] [--count] [--config=path]
                   input ...

The PyPI package formerly known as pep8. Checks Python source code style
against the PEP 8 style guide recommendations.

It allows you to report style errors and warnings in your Python source code.

usage: vulture [--exclude PATTERNS] [--ignore-decorators PATTERNS]
               [--ignore-names PATTERNS] [--make-whitelist] [--min-confidence INT]
               [--paths-of-dead-code PATTERNS] [--sort-by-size] [--version]
               [--whitelist FILENAME]
               [PATH ...]

Find dead Python code.
Vulture analyzes Python source files and reports unused code. It uses the AST
to find unused names (functions, methods, attributes, variables, imports).

Unused code:
  --min-confidence INT  Minimum confidence for code to be reported as unused [default: 60]
  --sort-by-size        Sort unused code by size
